title: A market early in the morning
summary: Describe what a busy outdoor market looks, sounds, and smells like at sunrise.
topic: A market early in the morning
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about an outdoor market early in the morning. Describe what you see, hear, and smell. Use present simple.
hintStarters: The market opens before the sun, so…
sampleText: I love the market in Marrakech early in the morning. It opens before the sun, so the first light of the day arrives slowly between the stalls. I go at six o'clock. The market is already full of people. Men carry heavy baskets of vegetables, and women open their stalls. I can hear sellers shout the price of tomatoes and onions. The air smells of spice, hot mint tea, and fresh bread. At one corner, an old man sells fresh fish on a bed of ice. At another corner, a boy pours tea into small glasses. The street is crowded, but everyone moves around each other. The morning light is soft and golden. I buy a glass of mint tea and a piece of bread. I stand near a wall and I watch the market wake up. I like this hour because the city is alive but the day is still quiet inside me.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ánh sáng đầu tiên của ngày

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Senses in the present simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Để mô tả giác quan, dùng động từ thường ở hiện tại đơn: 'I can hear', 'The air smells of', 'I watch'. Không dùng 'I am hearing' với nghĩa này.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: To describe senses, use ordinary present simple verbs: 'I can hear', 'The air smells of', 'I watch'. Don't use 'I am hearing' for this meaning.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'so' to give a result
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'so' nối hai mệnh đề khi mệnh đề thứ hai là kết quả: 'It opens before the sun, so the first light of the day arrives slowly between the stalls'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'so' joins two clauses when the second is a result: 'It opens before the sun, so the first light of the day arrives slowly between the stalls'.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi yêu cái chợ ở Marrakech vào sáng sớm. Chợ mở trước khi mặt trời lên, nên ánh sáng đầu tiên của ngày chầm chậm đến giữa các quầy hàng. Tôi đến lúc sáu giờ. Chợ đã đông người. Đàn ông xách những giỏ rau nặng, và phụ nữ mở quầy hàng của mình. Tôi nghe thấy người bán rao giá cà chua và hành. Không khí thơm mùi gia vị, trà bạc hà nóng và bánh mì tươi. Ở một góc, một ông cụ bán cá tươi trên một lớp đá. Ở góc khác, một cậu bé rót trà vào những cái ly nhỏ. Đường phố đông đúc, nhưng mọi người vẫn di chuyển quanh nhau. Ánh sáng buổi sáng dịu nhẹ và vàng. Tôi mua một ly trà bạc hà và một miếng bánh mì. Tôi đứng dựa vào tường và nhìn chợ thức dậy. Tôi thích giờ này vì thành phố sống động nhưng ngày vẫn còn yên tĩnh bên trong tôi.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'the market in Marrakech early in the morning'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What time does the writer go to the market?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I go at six o'clock'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the time the writer arrives.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Men carry heavy baskets of vegetables'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at what the men are doing.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the air smell of?
options: Spice, hot mint tea, and fresh bread
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The air smells of spice, hot mint tea, and fresh bread'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the list of three smells.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the old man sell?
options: Fresh fish on a bed of ice
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'an old man sells fresh fish on a bed of ice'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the corner detail about the old man.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'so' correctly to give a result?
options: The market opens early, but I go at six o'clock.
The market opens early, so I go at six o'clock.
The market opens early because I go at six o'clock.
The market opens early, so or I go at six o'clock.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'so' shows a result: the early opening is the reason, the visit is the result.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the option where 'so' shows a result of the first clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the boy pour into small glasses?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'a boy pours tea into small glasses'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find what the boy is pouring.

options: A glass of mint tea and a piece of bread
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I buy a glass of mint tea and a piece of bread'.

options: I can to hear the sellers.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'can', use the base verb: 'can hear'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer like this hour?
options: Because the city is alive but the day is still quiet inside
mcQuestions.explanation: The text gives the reflective reason: 'the city is alive but the day is still quiet inside me'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do many people enjoy markets more than supermarkets? What do you experience at a market that you can never have at a shop?
